actions.help: do not display information messages or progress status. Alternatively, this can be achieved by setting the MRTRIX_QUIET environment variable to a non-empty string.
use this number of threads in multi-threaded applications (set to 0 to disable multi-threading).
temporarily set the value of an MRtrix config file entry.
display this information page and exit.
do not delete intermediate files during script execution, and do not delete scratch directory at script completion.
manually specify the path in which to generate the scratch directory.
continue the script from a previous execution; must provide the scratch directory path, and the name of the last successfully-generated file.
The T1 image to be provided to FIRST
The lookup table file that the parcellated image is based on
Indicate that brain masking has been applied to the T1 input image
Consider the amygdalae and hippocampi as sub-cortical grey matter structures, and also replace their estimates with those from FIRST

argument_groups.title: Additional standard options for Python scripts

mrtrix.synopsis: In a FreeSurfer parcellation image, replace the sub-cortical grey matter structure delineations using FSL FIRST
mrtrix.author: Robert E. Smith (robert.smith@florey.edu.au)
citations: Patenaude, B.; Smith, S. M.; Kennedy, D. N. & Jenkinson, M. A Bayesian model of shape and appearance for subcortical brain segmentation. NeuroImage, 2011, 56, 907-922

Smith, S. M.; Jenkinson, M.; Woolrich, M. W.; Beckmann, C. F.; Behrens, T. E.; Johansen-Berg, H.; Bannister, P. R.; De Luca, M.; Drobnjak, I.; Flitney, D. E.; Niazy, R. NeuroImage, 2004, 23, S208-S219

description:
In a FreeSurfer parcellation image, replace the sub-cortical grey matter structure delineations using FSL FIRST.

References:

Patenaude, B.; Smith, S. M.; Kennedy, D. N. & Jenkinson, M. A Bayesian model of shape and appearance for subcortical brain segmentation. NeuroImage, 2011, 56, 907-922

Smith, S. M.; Jenkinson, M.; Woolrich, M. W.; Beckmann, C. F.; Behrens, T. E.; Johansen-Berg, H.; Bannister, P. R.; De Luca, M.; Drobnjak, I.; Flitney, D. E.; Niazy, R. NeuroImage, 2004, 23, S208-S219

Smith, R. E.; Tournier, J.-D.; Calamante, F. & Connelly, A. The effects of SIFT on the reproducibility and biological accuracy of the structural connectome. NeuroImage, 2015, 104, 253-265
